Notice And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Appear For An Ime, Appear For A Further Deposition
Off Calendar Sep-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEREMY PSTERNAK, TEL. NO. 643-0300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WARREN METLITZKY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3916 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Appear For An IME, Appear For A Further Deposition is Granted as to Form Interrogatories 6.2 , 6.3 and 2.3. Plaintiff must identify his injuries and provide information regarding his convictions, both felony and non felony. At deposition, Plaintiff must answer questions regarding his convictions, times and places or incarceration, and the medical conditions identified in the responses to FIs 6.2 and 6.3 or otherwise identified by Plaintiff as being at issue and those conditions that bear on Plaintiff's propensity to fall on his own. Plaintiff must appear for and IME, but it is limited to the medical conditions at issue and may not extend to Plaintiff's mental conditions. However Dr. Eyster may examine Plaintiff in a manner he deems most likely to yield relevant information about the medical conditions at issue. Plaintiff also to provide verifications. The court did not award sanctions. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Not reported = (301/EHG)

Notice And Motion To Quash Subpoenas For The Production Of Business Records, Or Alternatively For Protective Order
Off Calendar Sep-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEREMY PSTERNAK, TEL. NO. 643-0300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WARREN METLITZKY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3916 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF BROCK RASMUSSEN'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas For The Production Of Business Records, Or Alternatively For Protective Order - Protective order is granted. The subpoenas are overbroad. The order is without prejudice to more narrowly tailored subpoenas seeking information about the conditions Plaintiff identifies in his responses to Form Interrogatories 6.2 and 6.3 and those conditions that may bear on Plaintiff's propensity to fall on his own. The court did not award sanctions. Parties to meet and confer as to form of order. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Not reported = (301/EHG)
